profiles.title: Chief Information Officer / Leiter IT
priorities: Consolidate fragmented IT landscape inherited from regional utility mergers
Reduce IT cost-to-serve ratio from 4.5% to under 3% of revenue
Meet NIS2 and KRITIS compliance deadlines without disrupting grid operations
Enable digital products (smart metering, dynamic tariffs) on modern cloud platforms
pain_points: Legacy mainframe and SAP ECC systems approaching end-of-life with no clear migration path
Chronic shortage of cloud-skilled staff -- competing with hyperscalers and consulting firms for talent
Board pressure to cut IT spend while simultaneously accelerating digitalization
Fear of vendor lock-in with a single hyperscaler, but multi-cloud complexity is unmanageable internally
success_metrics: IT cost as percentage of revenue
Application availability (99.95%+ for grid-critical systems)
NIS2 / KRITIS audit pass rate
Time-to-market for new digital energy services
objections: We already have framework agreements with Accenture and Capgemini for cloud
T-Systems historically delivered on-prem hosting -- can you really do hyperscaler?
Our SAP S/4HANA migration is already planned with SAP directly
information_sources: Gartner Magic Quadrant for Managed Cloud Services
ISG Provider Lens for Cloud Transformation
BDEW (Bundesverband der Energie- und Wasserwirtschaft) IT working groups
Peer exchanges at E-world and Handelsblatt Energiegipfel
trigger_events: NIS2 transposition into German law with enforcement deadline
SAP ECC end-of-maintenance announcement
Board mandate to reduce IT costs by 20% within 2 years
Major outage or security incident at a peer utility

profiles.title: Chief Information Security Officer / IT-Sicherheitsbeauftragter
priorities: Achieve BSI KRITIS compliance and pass annual BSI audits without findings
Implement NIS2 requirements before October 2026 enforcement deadline
Protect OT/SCADA networks from increasing nation-state cyber threats
Establish sovereign data processing for energy trading and customer data
pain_points: OT security is fundamentally different from IT security, but leadership treats them as one
Cloud adoption outpaces the security team's ability to assess and certify new environments
Hyperscaler shared-responsibility models leave gaps that auditors flag every cycle
Skilled security analysts leave for better-paying positions at pure-play cyber firms
success_metrics: Number of open BSI audit findings (target: zero critical)
Mean time to detect and respond to incidents (MTTD/MTTR)
Percentage of workloads running in BSI C5-attested environments
NIS2 compliance readiness score
objections: How does T-Systems handle encryption key custody -- do we retain full control?
Your sovereign cloud still runs on shared infrastructure -- prove tenant isolation
We need BSI C5 Type 2 attestation, not just Type 1
information_sources: BSI IT-Grundschutz catalogs and KRITIS sector-specific guidance
UP KRITIS (government-industry cooperation for critical infrastructure)
Allianz fuer Cybersicherheit publications
ENISA threat landscape reports for energy sector
trigger_events: BSI audit with critical findings requiring remediation within 90 days
Ransomware attack on a German utility peer (e.g., Enercity 2022 pattern)
New BSI KRITIS-Verordnung amendments
Insurance carrier demanding higher security posture for cyber policy renewal

profiles.title: Bereichsleiter IT-Infrastruktur und Betrieb
priorities: Migrate 500+ workloads off aging data centers (owned or leased) before hardware end-of-life or lease expiry
Maintain 99.95% availability for grid management and energy trading platforms during and after migration
Build internal cloud operations competence while retaining existing staff -- upskill, not replace
Standardize infrastructure across business units acquired through mergers, eliminating duplicate platforms
pain_points: Data center infrastructure reaching end-of-life -- whether owned facilities need costly refresh or leases expire, the timeline is fixed
Team knows VMware and mainframes but has minimal Kubernetes or cloud-native skills, and retraining takes 12+ months
Every business unit has its own shadow IT stack from pre-merger days, making consolidation politically and technically painful
Broadcom VMware licensing changes are forcing urgent re-evaluation of virtualization strategy and driving up costs 200-300%
success_metrics: Number of workloads migrated per quarter
System availability (SLA achievement rate)
Infrastructure cost per workload
Incidents per change (change failure rate)
objections: Your migration methodology sounds great in slides -- show me a reference with our exact SAP landscape
We need guaranteed parallel-run support, not just a best-effort commitment
What happens to our operations team -- does T-Systems replace them or upskill them?
information_sources: Vendor RFI/RFP responses and reference calls
Gartner Peer Insights for managed infrastructure services
Internal POC results and benchmarks
IT operations team feedback from workshops
trigger_events: Data center lease expiry notification
VMware licensing cost increase (Broadcom acquisition impact)
Major production outage traced to aging infrastructure
Board approval of cloud-first strategy mandate

profiles.title: Chief Financial Officer / Finanzvorstand
priorities: Shift IT from CapEx-heavy data center investments to predictable OpEx cloud model
Free capital for core energy transition investments (renewables, grid expansion, storage)
Ensure IT investments show measurable ROI within 18 months
Reduce financial exposure from regulatory non-compliance penalties
pain_points: IT budget requests are opaque -- hard to tell transformation spend from keep-the-lights-on
Cloud promises cost savings but initial projects often overrun by 30-40%
NIS2 non-compliance fines (up to EUR 10M or 2% revenue) create material financial risk
Energy transition investments compete directly with IT modernization for board funding
success_metrics: Total cost of ownership reduction (3-year view)
CapEx-to-OpEx ratio improvement
IT spend as percentage of revenue
Regulatory compliance cost avoidance
objections: Show me a binding TCO comparison, not vendor marketing projections
What is the exit cost if we want to switch providers in 3 years?
Your pricing needs to fit our Energiewirtschaftsgesetz cost pass-through model
information_sources: Analyst TCO benchmarks (Gartner, ISG)
Peer CFO roundtables at energy industry events
Internal controlling and IT finance team analysis
Big Four advisory reports on cloud economics
trigger_events: Annual budget planning cycle (typically Q3 for following year)
Regulatory penalty risk assessment flagged by compliance team
Board directive to reduce IT costs as part of efficiency program
Failed audit that requires immediate remediation investment

profiles.title: Leiter Portfoliomanagement und Energiehandel
profiles.note: Present at all large utilities; trading desk size varies -- RWE/E.ON have large proprietary trading, EnBW/LEAG focus more on portfolio optimization
priorities: Low-latency compute for energy portfolio optimization and intraday trading
Real-time integration of renewable generation forecasts into dispatch and trading models
Regulatory compliance for REMIT, MiFID II (where applicable), and energy market transparency
Scale compute dynamically for intraday market volatility peaks and new balancing products
pain_points: On-premises portfolio and trading systems cannot scale for volatile intraday markets driven by renewables
Data sovereignty requirements prevent using US hyperscaler regions for trading and customer data
Legacy systems require manual intervention for cross-border trading reconciliation and Redispatch 2.0
Competing priorities with grid operations for shared IT infrastructure resources and budget
success_metrics: Trading platform uptime during market hours (target: 99.99%)
Latency for price signal processing
Time to deploy new trading algorithms to production
REMIT compliance audit results
objections: Can your sovereign cloud deliver the same latency as Frankfurt hyperscaler regions?
We need bare-metal performance for our quant models, not shared virtual machines
Our trading platform vendor only certifies on AWS -- how does your managed service work?
information_sources: European Energy Exchange (EEX) technology partnerships
Trading platform vendor roadmaps (e.g., Brady, Openlink/ION)
Bundesnetzagentur REMIT guidelines
Energy Trading Technology Conference
trigger_events: Extreme price volatility event exposing system limitations
New REMIT reporting requirement from Bundesnetzagentur
Trading platform vendor announcing cloud-first roadmap
Competitor gaining trading advantage through cloud-based analytics

profiles.role: Head of Grid Operations / Bereichsleiter Netzbetrieb
profiles.title: Leiter Netzbetrieb / OT-Verantwortlicher
priorities: Digitalize grid monitoring and predictive maintenance without compromising OT safety
Integrate distributed energy resources (solar, wind, storage) into grid management
Meet Bundesnetzagentur smart meter rollout mandates and Redispatch 2.0 process automation
Ensure air-gap isolation between IT cloud and OT SCADA networks while enabling edge analytics
pain_points: SCADA and grid control systems are 15-20 years old with no vendor upgrade path
IT cloud initiatives threaten OT network isolation that protects physical infrastructure
Smart grid data volumes are exploding but analytics capability is stuck in Excel
Regulatory mandates (Messstellenbetriebsgesetz) impose tight timelines with heavy penalties
success_metrics: Grid availability (SAIDI/SAIFI reliability indices)
Predictive maintenance accuracy (% of failures predicted)
Smart meter rollout progress vs. regulatory timeline
OT security incident count (target: zero)
objections: Cloud has no place near our OT systems -- convince me your hybrid approach is safe
How do you ensure that a cloud platform update never impacts grid control?
Our OT vendors (Siemens, ABB) have their own cloud platforms -- why add T-Systems?
information_sources: VDE/FNN (Forum Netztechnik/Netzbetrieb) technical standards
Bundesnetzagentur regulatory publications
OT vendor (Siemens, ABB, GE) technology roadmaps
BDEW Netzbetrieb working groups
trigger_events: Bundesnetzagentur deadline for smart meter gateway rollout
Grid stability incident caused by renewable intermittency
OT vendor announcing end-of-life for legacy SCADA platform
Successful pilot of cloud-based grid analytics at a peer DSO

profiles.title: Chief Digital Officer / Leiter Digitalisierung
profiles.note: Increasingly common at large utilities since 2022; sometimes combined with CIO role
priorities: Deliver customer-facing digital products (apps, dynamic tariffs, energy management portals)
Industrialize IoT and data analytics for asset management and grid optimization
Build a data platform that enables AI/ML use cases across the enterprise
Drive culture change from project-based IT to product-based digital teams
pain_points: Digital initiatives stall because underlying infrastructure is not cloud-ready
Data is trapped in silos across SAP, GIS, SCADA, and CRM systems with no integration layer
Competing with energy startups and tech companies for digital talent
Business units resist platform standardization, preferring their own best-of-breed tools
success_metrics: Number of digital products in production
Customer digital adoption rate (app usage, self-service quota)
Data platform coverage (% of enterprise data sources integrated)
Time from idea to minimum viable product
objections: We need a technology partner, not another outsourcer -- can T-Systems co-innovate?
Your cloud platform must support our data mesh architecture, not force a monolithic approach
How do you integrate with our existing Salesforce/SAP CX and IoT platforms?
information_sources: McKinsey/BCG energy digital transformation reports
Bitkom Digital Energy working group
AWS/Azure/GCP energy industry solution pages
Digital utility peer benchmarks (Accenture Utility Digital Maturity Index)
trigger_events: CEO setting public digital revenue targets at investor day
Competitor launching a successful consumer energy app
Data platform RFP triggered by failed analytics pilot
Paragraph 14a EnWG mandate requiring dynamic grid tariff infrastructure

stages: Internal needs assessment and business case development
RFP with detailed technical and commercial evaluation
Proof of value / pilot phase (4-8 weeks)
Contract negotiation including EVB-IT and framework terms
Vorstand / board approval for investments above EUR 500K
buying_process.decision_unit: IT steering committee (CIO chair, CFO, CISO, business unit heads)
buying_process.procurement_notes: Utilities with municipal or state ownership (e.g., EnBW, MVV) may be subject to Vergaberecht (public procurement law) requiring formal EU-wide tenders above EUR 215K threshold. Privately held utilities (E.ON, RWE) follow internal procurement policies but still use EVB-IT as standard contract framework.
